summary: Channel settings and model/provider migration is correctly implemented. The migrateChannels method properly gates token migration behind --migrate-secrets for Telegram, Discord, and Slack. The migrateModel method correctly maps provider types to PI_API, handles API key naming by provider, and respects --overwrite for existing values. Comprehensive tests cover all 12 validation assertions (VAL-CHAN-001 to 006, VAL-MODEL-001 to 006) plus additional provider-specific tests for OpenRouter and Anthropic.

issues.description: The channel migration handles Slack (SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_APP_TOKEN) and Signal (SIGNAL_ENABLED) but these are not covered by validation contract assertions. This is good forward-thinking but untested by the contract.
The ZAI_API_KEY provider mapping is not included in the switch-like if/else chain for API key naming. If a user has a Z.AI provider, their key would fall through to the generic 'API_KEY' env var.
issues.suggestedFix: Add an else if branch for 'zai' or 'z.ai' provider to write ZAI_API_KEY.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The migration script maps provider types to PI_API values (openai, anthropic, openrouter). The project also uses 'zai' as a provider (per AGENTS.md). The migration script should handle this mapping.

testCoverage.summary: Tests cover all channel assertions (VAL-CHAN-001 to 006): Telegram token with/without secrets, WhatsApp enabled, Discord token, .env conflict preservation, multiple channels. Model assertions (VAL-MODEL-001 to 006): PI_API from provider, PI_MODEL from source, API keys with/without secrets, OPENAI_BASE_URL, existing PI_MODEL preservation. Additional tests for OpenRouter and Anthropic providers.
